because.text: Accepted in full. Byte-novelty probe reproduced (two cosmetically-different check blobs, same verdict, both schema-valid, different WarrantIDs - the format cannot distinguish byte from claim novelty); rev 2 defines novelty by outcome fingerprint (ski@v1: term/expect/verdict/result hash; cmd@v1: evidence set/verdict/transcript with transcript REQUIRED for s7(b)). Tunnel closure now enumerated (prior-closure records + cited-blob set, no recursive blob parsing). Roots split into well-signed vs settlement-active; unadopted roots verify but settle nothing; adoption policy-authorized and jurisdiction-scoped. Rotation: rev 1's rationale contained a FALSE claim (both-keys does not stop silent rotation under outgoing-key compromise - the attacker mints the incoming key); rev 2 makes incoming sig proof-of-possession only, authorization = current-policy quorum of already-bound keys, emergency path bypasses the outgoing key. Key state derives from DAG order, ts never resurrects a key. Compatibility over-claim replaced with explicit activation rules (warrant_policy 0.3 blobs, settlement-inactive vs verification unchanged). Threshold grammar canonicalized. Rev 2 carries three new attack-asks; gate continues - at least one more review of rev 2 by a different model family before SPEC v0.3.

subject.note: Codex gate review 1 of GOV-001 rev 1: revise-before-adoption, 6 P1 + 2 P2, all three attack-asks broke -> rev 2 integrates every amendment
